reviewer_A_peer_SME.persona: Math PhD, AP Calc curriculum reviewer

issues.description: Outline lists arcsin and arctan basic-rule antiderivatives. These are LEGITIMATE basic rules under FUN 6.8 (the AP CED includes 1/(1+x²) → arctan and 1/√(1−x²) → arcsin as core antiderivative rules students must know). No issue.
The formal statement ∫f(g(x))·g'(x)dx = ∫f(u)du is mathematically correct and is the standard substitution theorem. Good.

Computation verified: x²+2x+5 = (x+1)²+4. Integrand 1/((x+1)²+4). With u=(x+1)/2 (so 2du = dx, (x+1)² = 4u²): denominator = 4u²+4 = 4(u²+1). Integrand·dx = (1/(4(u²+1)))·2du = (1/2)·du/(u²+1) → (1/2)arctan(u)+C = (1/2)arctan((x+1)/2)+C. Confirmed correct.
Computation: u=x²+1; x=0→u=1; x=1→u=2; ∫₁² u³ du = [u⁴/4]₁² = 16/4 − 1/4 = 15/4. Correct.
Polynomial division verified: x²+1 = (x−1)(x+1) + 2. Integral splits to (x+1) + 2/(x−1). Antiderivative x²/2 + x + 2 ln|x−1| + C. Correct.

reviewer_B_adversarial_student.persona: Top AP student probing for pedagogical misconceptions

issues.description: The 1/x → ln|x| rule in section 05 is correct. Make sure section 06 narration doesn't drop the absolute-value bars when discussing 1/x — many AP solutions accept ln(x) when domain is implicit but the rigorous AP answer is ln|x|. The current outline already handles this in 05 (explicit ln|x|), so just keep 06's wording from contradicting it.

Students often miss that the substitution u=(x+1)/2 gives du = dx/2 (so dx = 2du), and the factor of 1/4 in the denominator combined with the 2 from dx gives the 1/2 out front. The current outline glosses 'factor a four out... use u-sub' — at script-writing time, make sure narration explicitly mentions the dx = 2du step or students will be confused why the answer has a 1/2 instead of 1/4. Not a content error, just a pedagogical pacing risk.

reviewer_C_citation_checker.persona: Anti-hallucination fact checker against CED Unit 6 text

issues.description: CED Unit 6 reference lists FUN 6.8 (basic antiderivatives), FUN 6.9 (substitution), FUN 6.10 (long division and completing the square), and FUN 6.14 (selecting techniques). Outline covers all four. No BC-only topics (6.11 parts, 6.12 partial fractions, 6.13 improper) are smuggled in.

CED 'common AP traps' explicitly lists: forgetting +C, and substitution forgetting to change dx→du and update bounds. Outline addresses both. No claims, formulas, or numbers in the outline lack reference support.
Next module pointer 'Module 9 — Differential Equations' is consistent with the mapping table in references/ap-calc-ab-ced.md (Unit 7 → M9 area).

aggregate.reasoning: 0 critical, 2 minor — but both minors come from a single reviewer (B) and are pedagogical-pacing notes, not factual errors. Per AUTO_PIPELINE.md decision table: '≥2 reviewers say minor → revise'. Here only ONE reviewer flagged minors, so this falls under '0 critical, ≤1 minor reviewer' equivalent. Proceed to writer stage. The minors will be addressed inline by being mindful during script writing: keep ln|x| with bars in section 06, and explicit dx=2du step in section 17 narration.
